name: ExtensionPractitionerRole_Communication
title: R5: A language the practitioner (in this role) can use in patient communication (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `PractitionerRole.communication` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`PractitionerRole.communication` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `PractitionerRole.communication` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `PractitionerRole.communication` has a context of PractitionerRole based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `PractitionerRole`.
Element `PractitionerRole.communication` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: A language the practitioner (in this role) can use in patient communication (new)
element.comment:
Element `PractitionerRole.communication` has a context of PractitionerRole based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `PractitionerRole`.
Element `PractitionerRole.communication` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The structure aa-BB with this exact casing is one the most widely used notations for locale. However not all systems code this but instead have it as free text. Hence CodeableConcept instead of code as the data type.Note that for non-patient oriented communication, see Practitioner.communication.  Note that all 'person' type resources (Person, RelatedPerson, Patient, Practitioner) have a communication structure that includes preferences.  Role or service oriented resources such as HealthcareService and PractitionerRole only include languages that are available for interacting with patients.

element.short: A language the practitioner (in this role) can use in patient communication
element.definition: A language the practitioner can use in patient communication. The practitioner may know several languages (listed in practitioner.communication), however these are the languages that could be advertised in a directory for a patient to search.
element.comment: The structure aa-BB with this exact casing is one the most widely used notations for locale. However not all systems code this but instead have it as free text. Hence CodeableConcept instead of code as the data type.Note that for non-patient oriented communication, see Practitioner.communication.  Note that all 'person' type resources (Person, RelatedPerson, Patient, Practitioner) have a communication structure that includes preferences.  Role or service oriented resources such as HealthcareService and PractitionerRole only include languages that are available for interacting with patients.
